concerns.text: Success Criterion SC-002 imposes an MAE ceiling of ≤ 0.05, but the original idea never specified an MAE threshold; it only requested reporting MAE. Introducing this quantitative bound adds a new success condition not requested by the idea.

Success Criterion SC-006 mandates specific diagnostic thresholds (Shapiro‑Wilk p > 0.05, no heteroscedasticity, Spearman ρ ≥ 0.4). The idea’s methodology sketch mentioned a permutation test for significance but did not require these additional assumption checks, so this constitutes an over‑reach beyond the original research question.

Functional Requirement FR-011 requires a full suite of residual diagnostics (scatter plot, Shapiro‑Wilk, heteroscedasticity check, Spearman ρ). These diagnostics were not part of the idea’s stated methodology and therefore add work not requested.

The spec now states that the generated `dataset.csv` must be validated against a `dataset.schema.yaml` contract. The original idea did not mention any schema‑validation step; this is an engineering addition not required by the research question.

The spec references a `model.schema.yaml` requiring the architecture identifier `MLP-128-1`. The idea only described a “2‑layer MLP (≤ 100 k parameters)” without naming an identifier, so this contract requirement is beyond the scope of the original idea.

The spec adds an explicit SSoT declaration that `dataset.csv` is the single source of truth for downstream analyses. The idea never required a formal SSoT artifact; this is an additional constraint not present in the original scope.
